title: Infinite loop when compiling with bad enum/typedef syntax

features.name: typedef_enum_self_referential_base_type
features.description: Code should include a typedef enum declaration where the enum's base-type identifier is the same name as the typedef alias being defined, creating a self-referential type resolution cycle.

features.name: typedef_enum_undefined_base_type
features.description: Code should include a typedef enum declaration that uses an undeclared identifier as the enum's base type, which is syntactically invalid and should trigger a type-resolution error.

features.name: enum_typedef_name_shadowing_in_scope
features.description: Code should include two declarations in the same scope that use the same identifier: one as an enum tag reference in a typedef enum base type and one as the resulting typedef alias, causing a name collision.
